preview.summary: Norway, back at a World Cup after 28 years, face Senegal in a Group I meeting likely to shape the knockout race. Haaland's firepower against Senegal's physical, big-game know-how makes this a contest of contrasting strengths.
preview.homeForm: Norway, ranked 31st, won all eight qualifiers and scored 37 goals to reach their first World Cup since 1998.
preview.awayForm: Senegal, ranked 14th, finished qualifying unbeaten as the top-ranked African nation in the field.
preview.homeKey: Erling Haaland: 16 qualifying goals and Norway's all-time top scorer.
preview.awayKey: Nicolas Jackson: pace and finishing leading Senegal's attack.
storylines: Haaland and Odegaard headline Norway's long-awaited return
Senegal aim to build on a strong opener in the group
preview.h2h: The nations have met just once, a 2-1 Senegal friendly win in 2006, and never at a World Cup.
